message:
fix(ondisk,alloc,core): correct bitmap CRC32C to match kernel ext4 convention

Two bugs in bitmap checksum computation:

1. Group number was incorrectly chained as data prefix into the CRC.
   The kernel's ext4_block_bitmap_csum_set/ext4_inode_bitmap_csum_set
   simply does crc32c(s_csum_seed, bitmap_data, len) — no group number
   is mixed in. Group numbers are only used in group descriptor checksums.

2. Checksum length was always the full block size. The kernel checksums
   exactly clusters_per_group/8 bytes for block bitmaps and
   inodes_per_group/8 bytes for inode bitmaps. For groups where
   inodes_per_group/8 < block_size (common: 2048/8=256 vs 4096),
   the extra bytes were included in our CRC but not the kernel's.

Also:
- Added blocks_per_group and inodes_per_group to PersistCtx so
  stamp/verify functions have the correct lengths.
- GDT block calculation now uses sb.first_data_block + 1 instead
  of hardcoded if/else on block_size.
- Updated all callers in ffs-core, ffs-alloc, and test constructors.

The kernel_reference integration test ext4_bitmap_free_space_matches_kernel
now passes against real mkfs.ext4 1.47.2 images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
